title: LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE monochrome beauty brand mark: vectorize the supplied concept + build the full reversible variant system (master SVG, avatar, stacked lockup, one-color/mono-tint knockouts, print PNGs) with a font recommendation and palette recipe
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief "Modern Monochrome Logo Design": the client needs a "fresh brand mark that feels distinctly modern, sticks to a clean monochrome palette, and relies on crisp geometric shapes rather than illustrations or ornate flourishes"; it must "work just as well reversed out on dark backgrounds as it does in black on white, scale cleanly from social-media avatar size to large signage, and arrive to me in fully editable vector format (AI or SVG) alongside high-resolution PNGs"; "gradients, vintage treatments, or multicolored palettes are out of scope for this brief"; "Bold yet simple shapes that can be recognized instantly are the priority." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark and to fit slot V04 (vectorize_logo / vector / create / beauty_cosmetics): the raw brief as written implies drawing a mark from scratch, which is in the connector's BANNED bucket (no text-to-image, no bespoke letterform/illustration drawing). So the task is honestly scoped to the PRODUCTION / FINISHING half a freelancer actually runs in Adobe once a monochrome concept exists — the client (rebranded to the fictional beauty label "LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE") supplies a single approved flat black-on-white raster concept of the geometric monogram + wordmark lockup, and the connector chain CLEANS it, VECTORIZES it into the editable SVG master, and then ASSEMBLES the full reversible monochrome variant system the brief literally asks for: the black-on-white master, the reversed white-on-dark knockout, a tight avatar/PFP crop, a stacked lockup, a pure-black one-color print PNG, a single monochromatic-tint brand alt, plus a matching font recommendation and a monochrome swatch/palette recipe. No multicolor, no gradient, no vintage treatment is introduced (honoring the brief's exclusions). No deliverable is invented beyond the "vector + high-res PNG monochrome mark that reverses cleanly and scales from avatar to signage" the brief describes.
one_line_ask: Clean and vectorize LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE's supplied flat monochrome logo concept into an editable SVG master, then assemble the full reversible variant system — black-on-white master, white-on-dark knockout, avatar/PFP crop, stacked lockup, one-color black print PNG, a mono-tint alt, a monochrome palette recipe, and a font recommendation — all scalable from avatar to signage.
full_brief:
LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE is a fictional modern clean-beauty and skincare label preparing to launch across social, e-commerce, unit cartons, and large in-store signage. After an internal round we have approved ONE monochrome logo concept and now need it PRODUCTIONIZED into a fully editable, reversible brand-mark system — this is a finishing/assembly job, not a from-scratch design round.

We supply a single flat, high-contrast raster export of the approved concept: a crisp geometric monogram ("LM" resolved into an abstract, symmetric negative-space shape) sitting above the wordmark "LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE" set in a clean modern condensed sans, rendered pure black on a near-white background, no gradients, no illustration, no flourishes. The concept scan is slightly skewed and the background is an off-white (#F5F5F3), not true white.

Deliverables, all chained in the Adobe connector:
1) Ingest our supplied concept PNG and get a clean working copy — straighten the slight skew and auto-tone so the black is dense and the edges are true.
2) Knock the off-white background out to true transparency so the trace has no halo/fringe.
3) Vectorize the cleaned lockup into a clean, fully editable SVG — this is the MASTER vector (basis for the requested AI/SVG editable file + high-res PNG exports).
4) Produce the REVERSED knockout: a white-on-dark version of the mark that reads perfectly when reversed out on dark packaging and signage (the brief's key requirement).
5) Derive a tight AVATAR / social-PFP version from the monogram alone (square, centered) that stays recognizable at avatar size.
6) Build a STACKED lockup crop for narrow / vertical placements.
7) Produce a ONE-COLOR pure-black print PNG (for single-color foil/deboss and small-format print).
8) Produce a single MONOCHROMATIC brand-tint alt (one hue only — no multicolor, honoring "clean monochrome palette") for tonal applications.
9) Recommend a brand font family + heading/supporting pairing that matches the modern condensed wordmark tone.
10) Emit a MONOCHROME palette recipe (black / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts) as the color-palette deliverable.

Hard constraints (honor the brief AND connector ground truth): stay strictly monochrome — no gradients, no multicolor, no vintage/distress treatment. Everything must reverse cleanly, scale from a social-media avatar up to large signage, and arrive as an editable vector plus high-res PNGs. No from-scratch logo drawing, no generative fill, no AI object removal, no compositing — the supplied concept is the single source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The requested AI/EPS export and the printed spec sheet are human/format-export finishing items layered on top of the connector-produced SVG + PNG masters.

inputs.name: lumiere_menage_concept.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, high-contrast monochrome logo concept export for a modern clean-beauty skincare brand. A bold, minimal, geometric monogram mark resolving the letters 'L' and 'M' into a single symmetric abstract shape built from crisp straight lines and negative space (no illustration, no ornament, no flourishes) sits centered above a clean modern condensed sans-serif wordmark reading 'LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE' in all caps, with a thin small tagline 'CLEAN BEAUTY' beneath. Everything is pure black (#111111) on a near-white #F5F5F3 background — strictly one color, no gradients, no shading, no drop shadow, no color. The whole lockup is very slightly rotated about 2 degrees clockwise as if exported at a tilt, with faint compression softness at the edges. Crisp legible type, hard geometric edges, high resolution 2400x2000px.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as a real client-supplied 'approved concept, only have it as a flat PNG' hand-off: a genuine ~2 degree skew so auto_straighten has real work, an off-white (#F5F5F3) rather than pure-white background so remove_background + auto_tone are warranted, mild compression softness, and hard high-contrast geometric edges + legible type so image_vectorize yields clean monochrome paths and select_by_prompt / crop_to_bounds can target the monogram vs the wordmark. Strictly monochrome per the brief (no gradients/multicolor) so the vectorize and the reversed knockout stay clean. gemini-3-pro-image is chosen for faithful, correctly-spelled logo text over a photoreal model.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_master.svg
outputs.spec: Clean, fully editable vectorized MASTER of the full monochrome lockup (LM monogram + LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE wordmark + tagline) as scalable SVG with crisp geometric paths on transparent background; the print/signage-ready master from which the AI/EPS + high-res PNG exports derive. Strictly monochrome black.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_reversed_white_on_dark.png
outputs.spec: Reversed knockout: the mark converted to solid white (via monochromatic tint, high-lightness single hue) so it reads over dark packaging/signage, hi-res PNG — the brief's key 'must reverse out on dark backgrounds' requirement.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_avatar_pfp.png
outputs.spec: Tight square avatar / social-PFP crop of the LM monogram alone, centered with padding, transparent RGBA hi-res PNG, recognizable at small avatar size.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_stacked_lockup.png
outputs.spec: Stacked / vertical layout crop of the master lockup, cropped to true content bounds, transparent hi-res PNG for narrow and vertical placements.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_oneclr_black.png
outputs.spec: One-color pure-black (#111111) version of the lockup (via monochromatic-tint black conversion) on transparency, hi-res PNG for single-color foil/deboss and small-format print.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_mono_tint.png
outputs.spec: Single monochromatic brand-tint alternate of the lockup (one hue only, no multicolor) for tonal applications, hi-res PNG.

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_font_recommendation.json
outputs.spec: Recommended brand font family + heading/supporting pairing and usage notes matched to the modern condensed wordmark tone (font_recommend output).

outputs.name: lumiere_menage_palette_recipe.md
outputs.spec: Monochrome palette / usage recipe note (black #111111 / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts, clear-space + minimum-size notes) written locally as the color-palette deliverable; the printed swatch sheet is a human export layered on top.

connector_workflow.note: Begin upload of the client's supplied flat monochrome concept PNG into Adobe storage; returns an upload target/session.
Finalize the upload to a stable presigned asset URI the image_* tools can consume — the single source of truth for the chain.
Correct the ~2 degree export skew so the geometric edges are true before tracing — straight edges vectorize cleaner.
Auto-tone the straightened concept to normalize contrast and drive a dense, clean black-vs-white separation for the trace.
Knock the off-white #F5F5F3 background to true transparency (default transparent-PNG cutout, no reconstruction) so the vectorize has no halo/fringe. Hub cleaned master.
Vectorize the cleaned monochrome lockup into a clean, fully editable SVG = the MASTER vector deliverable (basis for AI/EPS/PNG).

Monochromatic-tint the transparent mark to solid white (single hue, high lightness) -> reversed white-on-dark knockout, the brief's key reverse-out requirement (deliverable). One hue only, honoring the monochrome-only rule.
Select the 'LUMIERE MENAGE' wordmark + tagline text region by prompt (mask only) so it can be knocked out to isolate the LM monogram for the avatar.

Solid-fill the selected wordmark sub-region white (solid fill only, driven by the wordmark_mask) to knock it out, leaving just the isolated LM monogram. Allowed sub-region fill, not a whole-background replace.
Crop the isolated monogram tight to its true content bounds so it centers cleanly for the avatar.
Crop+resize the tight monogram to a centered square social-PFP / avatar asset that stays recognizable at small size (deliverable).
Crop the cleaned master to its true content bounds -> tight stacked/vertical lockup for narrow placements (deliverable).
Monochromatic-tint to a pure-black (#111111) single-tone conversion -> one-color print version for foil/deboss/small-format (deliverable). Single tone, no multicolor.
Apply a single-hue monochromatic tint (one color only, no multicolor -> honors 'clean monochrome palette') -> tonal brand alt (deliverable).
Recommend the brand font family + heading/supporting pairing matched to the modern condensed wordmark tone (deliverable).

Emit the monochrome palette / usage recipe (black #111111 / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts + clear-space & minimum-size notes) as the color-palette deliverable. A single image_fill_area call cannot produce a 4-block swatch sheet (one masked color per call), so the palette is recorded as a recipe note here.

chaining_note: Single connected chain from one supplied concept: ingest (1-2) -> clean pipeline straighten (3) -> auto-tone (4) -> remove_background (5) yields the hub concept_clean_transparent, which fans out to vectorize master (6), reversed white knockout (7), monogram isolation (8 select mask -> 9 fill) -> avatar crop chain (10 crop-to-bounds -> 11 crop-and-resize), stacked lockup (12), one-color black (13), mono-tint (14); font_recommend (15) from the master SVG; and local palette recipe (16) from the produced masters. Every post-ingest step consumes a prior named output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (16 connector calls, 12 distinct Adobe tools plus one local step, past the >=10 / >=6 floor) with a genuine wide mix across asset ingest, the image_* raster/vector family, font, and a local recipe emit. Difficulty is in correct ordering (straighten + tone + bg-removal before vectorize for a clean monochrome trace), a real isolate-the-monogram sub-chain (select_by_prompt -> solid-fill knockout -> crop-to-bounds -> crop-and-resize), and reusing one cleaned hub to spawn six variations. Stays strictly inside connector limits and honors the brief's monochrome-only exclusions; the vector emerges from image_vectorize, the reversed/one-color/mono-tint variants from single-hue monochromatic tint (not multicolor, not gradient), and the palette guide from a local recipe note because a single image_fill_area call cannot author a multi-color swatch sheet. Mostly autonomous ([C] headless throughout, one [L] local recipe emit).

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / generative-fill; no text-to-image or from-scratch logo/letterform drawing (the monochrome concept is a SUPPLIED input; the connector only cleans, vectorizes, and re-tints it); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting (image_remove_background outputs a transparent-PNG cutout only with no pixel reconstruction, and image_fill_area does solid-color fill of a prompt-selected sub-region only); no bespoke font design (font_recommend only recommends existing families); no compositing; no unreachable external account. image_vectorize is used exactly as allowed (raster -> vector trace).
reverify.notes: Corrections applied vs the authored spec: (a) Removed the two image_apply_color_overlay steps (original n7/n13) — that tool creates a solid fill layer, cannot set opacity, and on a transparent PNG risks filling the whole canvas; replaced both with image_apply_monochromatic_tint, whose documented purpose is exactly monochrome/B&W conversion and single-hue tinting (n7 = solid white for the reverse-out knockout, n13 = pure-black single-tone for the one-color print), keeping strictly one hue per the monochrome-only brief. (b) Replaced the original swatch-sheet image_fill_area step (old n15) with local_write_recipe_note: a single image_fill_area call fills ONE mask region with ONE solid color and requires a maskURI, so it cannot author a 4-block black/grey/off-white/paper swatch sheet; the palette is honestly emitted as a local recipe note (the printed swatch sheet is a human export layered on top). Each remaining potentially-risky step stays in-bounds: (5) image_remove_background produces a transparent-PNG cutout only, not a reconstructed background. (6) image_vectorize traces the already-cleaned high-contrast monochrome raster into paths — a trace of a supplied concept, not a generated-from-scratch logo. (7)(13)(14) image_apply_monochromatic_tint applies a SINGLE hue (white / pure-black / one brand tint) — single-color, no gradient, no gen content, honoring the brief's monochrome-only rule. (8) image_select_by_prompt returns a selection mask only; it does not remove or reconstruct. (9) image_fill_area is SOLID-COLOR fill of the prompt-selected wordmark sub-region (allowed sub-region fill, explicitly NOT a whole-background replace and NOT fed a transparent-PNG alpha as a mask). (16) local_write_recipe_note is an [L] local emit, not an image op. workflow_nature=create is satisfied: the variant SYSTEM is ASSEMBLED from the single supplied concept part via non-generative transforms. Counts consistent: tool_call_count=16 matches the 16 connector_workflow steps; distinct_adobe_tools=12 counts distinct adobe-server tools only, excluding the one [L] local_write_recipe_note step (image_apply_monochromatic_tint reused x3, image_crop_to_bounds reused x2 are each counted once). Slot-match: category=vectorize_logo, output_modality=vector (master SVG is the vector deliverable via image_vectorize), workflow_nature=create, vertical=beauty_cosmetics, IP-clean fictional brand LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE. slot_id preserved as V04.
